reviews.review_text:
This is possibly my favorite Assassin's Creed title of all time...
The setting, atmosphere, gameplay, riddles, tombs, Trials of the Gods, DLC content, graphics, and characters. There is so much to do in this game, so many unique experiences to be had and a good amount of replay ability. 10/10.

  Everything is gorgeous. The lighting especially has always stood out to me for a game released in 2017 with absolutely no Ray-Tracing (RTX) support, it's stunning. You can tell the developers put a ton of love and care into meticulously designing the world and graphical/lighting systems. This game is definitely a showcase for its time. (I played @1440P, Very High/High, HDR ON, 60 FPS VSYNC ON for reference.) Everything felt smooth.

The combat while being initially hard to learn is extremely rewarding when mastered with lots of variety in weapon type, both melee and ranged. My first playthrough I abused Sickle Swords and Spears, my second I started exclusively using a Heavy Blunt and Twin Blades as a challenge until beating the Trials of the Gods and then settling on the Regular Sword and Scepter. each weapon type having its own unique playstyle to master, with many different variations in stats and visual appeal to chose from.

Additionally, there is almost always multiple ways to approach a situation, you can go in "Guns Blazing" or stealthily for many different enemy encounters and are not punished for clearing areas how you want.

Nightmare Difficulty is quite challenging at first, but once you get good gear, or upgrade your equipment and abilities enough it's very doable. If you are after a challenge with this game I would highly recommend the Trials of the Gods. The Phylakes are quite tough and the DLC versions of them are a bit tougher. The Gladiator Arena is very fun as well and has some challenging bosses, plus you can compare scores with friends.

  The story is fantastic, another massive strong point of this game. Bayek, the protagonist, is driven and the game does a good job of keeping his motivations clear. The supporting cast and antagonists help to flush out the story well.

  I firmly enjoyed playing The Curse Of The Pharaohs DLC and completing all the riddles in the base game and DLC, Some of which were genuine challenges. Don't google them! 😂 

  The meh:
LOTS OF QUESTS, 183 for full completion. many of which are fetch quests.

The Hidden Ones DLC is forgettable.

Trials of the Gods, while fun and extremely challenging, makes you wait a week between successful attempts to obtain all the gear, which you have to repeatedly kill both the easy and hard god 4 times in total to obtain all the gear. That's 8 fights over the course of 4 weeks. 2 fights per week. You CAN repeat the fights as many times as you want its just tedious. (I would wait until you're max level as the gear drops at whatever level you are and it's quite good. Upgrading gear is expensive)

UPGRADES ARE EXPENSIVE, and there's lots of gear, choose wisely.

  I can't put everything in this review, truly this game is one of a kind, I thoroughly enjoyed it. Go explore Egypt as a Medjay, role-play, challenge yourself with the combat and Nightmare Mode, clear massive bases completely unseen. Take in the sights, solve some riddles, defeat the gods! have fun and enjoy. At time of writing "Assassin's Creed Origins - Gold Edition" (All DLC included version of the game) is on sale for $14.99USD this game will be a decade old next year and it still holds up today. $15USD is well worth it for this game.
Just because I can't open a Ubisoft Account I can't play this game, screw you Ubisoft.

Let me begin this review by admitting I never play anything like this. I normally play Animal Crossing, Stardew valley and games where there are cartoon animals. The game has been off and on my wishlist a lot last year with constant ‘’should I or shouldn’t I’’ mainly because I never played anything like this before and kind of doubted if I could even do it. Eventually it was on sale with all its dlc for 6 euros, so my friends told me I couldn’t get a better deal on this, and I decided that if it wasn’t a fun game for 6 euros, it would be an acceptable loss. 

What really drew me in every time is the setting of this game. The Ancient Egypt setting and that the game looks so beautiful and alive and lush. It is like watching a movie instead of playing a game, to me. It also has been that fascination of mine since I was a little kid, so yeah, a good lure. 

The game itself surprised me in the best way possible. It’s my first assassin’s creed game, and if all their stories are written like that, I might try out others as well. The story is not what I expected at all. I expected to see an arrogant mean man with who I couldn’t relate at all who kills for glory and fun and instead got a haunted father who lost everything and tries to get justice in a world deprived of just that. The switching between modern and ancient times was confusing sometimes, but I grew to love it after I understood what was happening. The story had for me, a lot of hits and times where you are sucked into the game and where for me the cozy gamer left for a moment, and the assassin snuck back out.

I also want to comment that I am no way the best fighter, but this is the beauty of picking up an olderish game (still 2018 is pretty recent) if you make a mistake in this game: GOOD NEWS :) Someone else did the EXACT same thing before you and posted about it online! Any temple, any missing collectible and achievement was just a quick google and reading along. For example, I walked into a bathouse via the FRONTDOOR to assasinate someone and didn’t know how to get out, until I read somewhere else it had a ceiling entrance (but who suspects the guy entering via the front door? no one! It is truly genius! Not a skill issue!) The game has multiple difficulty settings so even if you aren’t the greatest fighter, the story is very good, the locations are beautiful and the lore, well, I wasn’t there in Ancient Egypt but from what I’ve seen, they’ve truly done it justice. 

An adventure worth losing yourself into for a good amount of hours and giving you an adventure you won’t soon forget.

A fantastic experience! The storyline is gripping, the gameplay feels smooth and satisfying, and the graphics are absolutely stunning. Character customization is well-crafted, and the historical accuracy—especially in names, locations, and world-building—adds a surprising amount of depth.

A must-play for anyone who enjoys stealth-platformers or is a fan of the Assassin’s Creed series.

I loved this game when it came out. I reinstalled last week. Ubisoft locks my account every time I start the game. I either have to reset my password or contact support and wait for them to unlock. Itll work for an evening, then account locked next day. It keeps happening. They never tell me how to prevent it. I just get an email saying it is unlocked. I would suggest not buying any ubisoft products.

I like games that have historical accuracy in such a way you can even learn something from it. 

This game has the best discovery tour implementation of all in the series. It was just simple, fully narrated content that explained the subjects of ancient egypt, accompanied with real life depictions of artifacts and schematics. 

The later games were just annoyingly interactive, had only a portion narrated, forced you to do tests, or fictional quests and such.
The storyline and the graphics are good and the game is also well optimized

This game is so amazing. I loved the map it looked absolutely gorgeous and the story was great. Bayek's voice acting was really good in my opinion, he did a great job. The game explains a lot about Ancient Egypt, which was very interesting. 10/10 totally recommend!!

I would do neutral but I cant so overall its a net gain, why do half the side quests make me walk like a slow ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥ instead of letting me run, the next objective is 0ver 100m away and you are making me walk and "dodge" to be faster, overall still fun and enjoyable, you don't miss any world building if you skip side quests

EDIT:
fun game story does pick up and become more engaging and fun. i think the protagonists aren't the most engaging but that's okay. if you like assassins creed play this game it is enojyable, i do however stand by my hatred of the side quests that involve me escorting someone, I hate that these quests take 5 minutes just because the cpu is programmed to walk so slow and make it take long, however this is an older game so whatever, all in all, a fun a game and i do love this series

Assassin's Creed Origins is one of the best games in the Assassin's Creed series. It takes the player to the world of ancient Egypt, which is incredibly beautifully created and full of historical objects, secrets and exciting adventures. The main character of the game, Bayek, is a charismatic and emotionally powerful character, whose story of revenge and the fight against the ancestors of the Templars is captivating from beginning to end. Of particular note is the vast open world, where you can explore pyramids, deserts, oases and such impressive cities as Alexandria. The combat system is modernized, dynamic and provides more freedom than in previous parts of the series, while RPG elements allow you to develop your own style of play. The game's graphics still look great, and the atmosphere is complemented by an excellent soundtrack. Many players believe that it was Assassin's Creed Origins that best revived the series and introduced fresh ideas, while maintaining the essence of Assassin's Creed. Rating: 9/10 – a great story, an unforgettable world of ancient Egypt, and an exciting gaming experience that I would definitely recommend to any adventure game fan.
Spent 60 minutes running around the world aimlessly, contemplating why i bought this game.

TBH, aint worth buying at full price.
That said I got it on a beefy discount.

Whats in the box?
-Good graphics
-Fairly big map
-♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sand everywhere
-Mid combat
-Mid-ish story
-Mid enemies, only a handful really feel difficult, and my HP was locked on 1/3.
-Your wife leaves you, she was probably sick of all the sand tho.
-Interestingly enough,they added a "Tour" section which walks you through how stuff in ancient egypt worked, pretty interesitng if you're into history, boring as balls if you're not.

That said the game is enjoyable and easy to 100% within 75 Hours.
Just not quite worth the pricetag imo.


PS. This was my first AC game, i'm playing the others now so this review does not consider the other games in the series at all.
Second time playing through the game as a refresher. Definitely fun and love Egyptian era of history.

I purchased this game, as well as many other Assassin's Creed/EA games, well over a year ago.  I'd probably had an EA Connect account a decade or longer ago on XBox.  In any case, earlier today, I watched a fascinating documentary about ancient Egypt on youtube and I had a sudden hankering to get back into Assassin's Creed Origins and experience that world again.

So I click on the game's icon on my desktop.  EA Connect pops up, I'll need to sign in before I can play the game.  Hmm, okay.  Input my email address, try a password, it doesn't work.  Try a similar permutation of that password that I've had to use since login requirements have become more draconian.  Doesn't work.  Run through about 6 permutations of two passwords that I know I've used before, since I haven't logged into EA Connect for at least 2 years.  None work.  Maybe it was my second email account, though?  Who can remember?  I try logging in twice with my secondary email account, but after two attempts with variations of my password, it says my account has been suspended (temporarily) due to too many unsuccessful login attempts!  It recommends waiting for some (unspecified amount of) time or resetting a new password.  I go into my email, go through the old rigamarole, and input what I want to be my new password.  It is an unacceptable password because, although it has upper-case letters, lower case letters, and numbers included, and it between 8 and 16 digits, it does NOT meet EA's final requirement of having a symbol included, as well!  Unbelievable.  Because I had forgotten this one little particular detail about EA login requirements compared to every other account I have, I was now locked out of my account and with no access to ANY of the EA content I had purchased.

I input the new password that made the most sense to me, plus added a single symbol at the end of it, retyped it a second time to confirm it, and.......You cannot use that password because that is a password that you have already used on this account recently.  Unbelievable.  Once I knew that I had to include a symbol in the password for EA, I had re-guessed my own years-old password on the first attempt.

I reset the password to something similar, closed out of it, and went on Reddit to see how long "temporary account suspensions" from EA are for failed login attempts.  The consensus seems to be 45-90 minutes.

In 45-90 minutes, I need to go to the gym, get some supper, do some evening chores, and just generally move on with my life.  Although I was inspired quite specifically to explore ancient Egypt again today, it now appears that it will be completely impossible for me to do so because EA, in their infinite wisdom, have added so many superfluous steps just to access their decade-old, single-player, offline games that a few failed login attempts to their unnecessary intermediary app between you and the games you've purchased from them send you into a Kafka-esque nightmare of friction that ultimately prevents you from doing the very simple thing that you set out meaning to do.

This is the first, and so far only, Steam review I've ever written, but EA has pushed me to this.  Based on my experience, I cannot, in good conscience, recommend Assassin's Creed Origins, whatever it's particular merits as a game may be, until EA as a company becomes a much better version of itself and fixes this insanity.  I shouldn't need to log in to your particular intermediary app to play my game that I paid for.

[h1] By the Gods of Egypt, The legend of Siwa [/h1] 
[b] Assassin's Creed Origins grabbed me from the very first second and never let go. It's easily one of the best games I've ever played.
Stepping into Ancient Egypt felt so incredibly real, and the way they brought the Roman and Greek occupation to life is just mind-blowing.
The world is as smooth as butter, the gameplay is pure adrenaline, and Bayek is a total legend.
It's the ultimate experience for any history buff out there. [/b]  
[b] [h3] My final verdict? Ten out of ten, no questions asked! [/h3][/b]
This game is great to play alone and want something emotional and challenging (in some aspects)

PROS:
I really enjoyed the story, for knowing how the origins of the creed happened and all that, and of bayek of siwa as well with what happened to him. I heard a lot of negative review regarding the combat when moving from A.C unity to Origins hearing it will be bad and not playable. I must say, I almost quitted the game around the 1 hour mark for not knowing how the combat works at all but then I decided to give it a bit more time and honestly it wasnt as bad as everyone said and I really enjoyed it. As for the eagle vision. in unity it was a press of button and it will show all enemies and loot. whilst here u had to hover from above using your eagle senu. didnt like it at first but it aint bad either.

CONS:

What I didnt like about the game is how it forced you to do side missions. you've to do them so you can level up and be able to fight high level npc/boss. So the travel became annoying aswell as I was going around doing side missions on my Camel mostly instead of climbing buildings and all that. but overall these reasons dont outweight the things I enjoyed. one last thing. this game didnt take me 56 hours to finish. I was Afk a lot. so I would guess from 36 to 42 hours is my real playtime.


(One last note. this game has nudity and I forgot. not that it matters its just that you can see the female chest. so if I were playing in the living room then it could've been a problem so yea...I dont think there is anything else more than that.)

assasins creed origins is by far the best of the rpg like AC games and it shows

Bayeks voice actor is phenomenal and him as a character is extremely fun to play
the story while simple is extremely well made and tailored along with the insane amount of detail in the world 

the side quests are insanely fun and while they may get repetitvie are wroth completing

the only problem i find with the game is that if you want to 100% it you need to purchase the DLC content as almost 40% of all achievements in the game are acquirable only through the dlc

this game is pretty fun and over all worth the money great story telling  this is my pc specs over all if you want a smooth gameplay you gonna have to tune down your graphic none the less it will lag bit but is normal for a pc that low end

Operating System: Windows 11
CPU Brand: Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-4460 CPU @ 3.20GHz
RAM: 16 GB
Video Card: Radeon RX 570 Series
Primary VRAM: 4 GB

So for starters, it's really grindy. I mean REALLY grindy. Trying to punch up is generally a waste of time. The world is beautiful, the story is decent, Bayek is a good character. That said, for me, the game randomly started booting on the wrong monitor, and I couldn't get it to stop, even after switching the device in game, on system, AND unplugging the incorrect monitor entirely. Between that, which basically broke my game, and having to grind for hours after every 2-3 story missions, I can't recommend this version at the very least. I wouldn't recommend it as a whole at this point. It's been a generally frustrating experience when it doesn't work, and an unsatisfying one when it does.

Harto harto de que querer jugar y no poder porque estos.. es.tu...pídos... de Ubisoft ponen que hay que estar se logeando por un juego que tú pagaste , voy a desinstalar porque me cansé de estar con lo mismo juro no volver a comprar un juego de esta empresa e instó a los demás a seguir el ejemplo ojalá quiebren pronto. Hijos d....ya estoy harto sigue lo mismo eh cambiado la contraseña y sigue suspendida porque pagas por los juegos pero tienes que estar logeando a la plataforma de ellos para poder jugar tu juego NO COMPREN JUEGOS DE ESTA COMPAÑÍA por eso no compre el Valhalla aún cuando estaba a 4 dólar por esto mismo porque ya se cómo son estos delincuentes

At first i won't be able to start the game as i've installed it on my D drive instead of the C drive but after reinstalling it on the D drive the game is smooth no more problem arises.

About the review of this game, this game is fun, interactive and don't feel repeatative as the side quest and mission keep you engaged while the enviorment in this game is mesmerising.

It deserve straight 4.5/5 🤩

I played about 5 hours of this game and, unfortunately, it didn't hold my interest. The graphics and recreation of Ancient Egypt are impressive, with beautiful environments and plenty of detail. However, the story feels weak and failed to engage me. The side quests quickly became repetitive and more annoying than enjoyable, while many of the dialogues felt flat and poorly written. Progression also seemed slow, making it difficult to feel rewarded for the time invested. While the visuals are excellent, the gameplay and storytelling weren't strong enough to keep me playing.
Sometimes the horse call won't work, and mid fight, the enemy tag won't switch to the next person. Overall, the game is awesome and I play it a lot. I also use it on PS5.
The best AC RPG ever. Doesn't lose its identity of an Assasin's Creed game unlike other AC RPG games that followed this game.
Great, but im not happy with the fact that it still requires ubisoft connect to launch
A beautiful journey through ancient Egypt. Assassin’s Creed Origins offers the perfect blend of storytelling, exploration, and action, making every moment feel special. Overall a good game.

As a lover of the franchise, this was easily the worst AC I have ever played. 

PROS:
* The open world is beautiful. A fully realized ancient egypt with tons of detail in the architecture, marble/stone work, and statues. And a serious commitment to bringing history to life, as always the team nailed it on this front.
* Your eagle is actually an essential and meaningful part of exploration and quest design. It can be a little monotonous switching back and forth but for me it was fine. Your eagle actually perches on your arm when you're at rest.
* The street cats are pretty cute when they run up to you and stand between your legs.
* Darkness is actually dark and requires the use of a torch.
* There is one cosmetic armor set you can get free from a quest pretty early on that looks great. Just one.

CONS:
* Melee combat is an insufferable slog. Once an enemy catches you in a combo it is impossible to get out. Your dodge is not nearly a far enough distance to be useful. You just have to get butt-f**ed. They advertise heavy attacks to break guard, although with certain weaponry heavy attacks wont break guard they just stagger you. With other weaponry, heavy attacks also send the opponent a bit backwards out of your melee range so you have to dodge to be able to hit them but by that time the enemy has recovered and will instantly counter attack you. So what was the point? I just resorted to bows or cheesing the stealth system. Maybe I just suck at the melee combat but it is difficult for me to see how anyone could enjoy it.  
* The story is mediocre and the side quests are okay. I was not at all invested in this story as many of the characters they want you to care about, you spend maybe 5 mins with and then they become a driving force for the player char's motivation. Meanwhile I'm sitting here with a stone cold expression on my face completely apathetic to this kid who just died.
* Every bandit and town guard is a world-class champion, olympic level horse archer and marksman who is able to track your dodges and parkour with expert precision. Super fun. Especially when they gang mob you and are all shooting arrows and ballista bolts into your back.
* High level bandits wait for you on the side of the road to kill you on your way back to turn in a quest.
* You have to literally genocide native wildlife populations just to upgrade your gear a few times.
* The player character as well as many of the NPCs did not look or sound Egyptian, they looked and sounded central or south African. Just a little weird. 
* Chariots and horses are pretty buggy.
* Armor choices were so sparse and ugly. I used one set of armor I got near the beginning of the game from a quest with a cool helmet and cuirass design and just stuck with it throughout. I periodically checked the clothes/armor vendor and was disappointed every time.
* Climbing pyramids was surprisingly annoying and tedious.

After finishing Valhalla and Odyssey, both of which I really love and have put hundreds of hours into, I decided to play Origins to immerse myself in another part of this historical fiction series. But I came out of it hating it. Between wanting to do as little of the side quests as possible in order to level myself for the main story or explore any of the question marks on the world map and grinding for resources and exp, this game was a painful exercise in patience. I don't even know who I would recommend this game to. As someone who has loved every other AC game I've played I can't even say 'if you're a fan of the franchise, you should pick it up...' because I don't think you should. Either way I'm happy they improved gameplay in later entries with this as their stepping stone. If you do end up buying it on deep discount, avoid combat as much as possible.

Decent game but put off by UBISOFT Launcher.

The Ubisoft launcher will allow you to log in on the web but will tell you the same username/pw combo is false when using the launcher.

Step1: got to web and change password to existing password.

Step 2: mess about as Ubisoft require you to go back and forth with 2fa 3 times, despite not being able to recogbnize a correct password in the first place.

Step 3: verify yourself a few more times.

step4 : prepare to do this ♥♥♥♥ again next time you want to play.


Ubisoft launcher is pain and will not remember your password.

I stayed away from this game for a long time since I did not like the switch to RPG style combat. However, curiosity got the best of me a few months ago and boy was I in for a treat. Bayek's story reminds me of Ezio's story. The voice acting is incredible and it genuinely feels like a movie you can play. I'm still not a huge fan of the combat or upgrade systems, but the game is worth playing just for the story, visuals and voice acting. This is the Ubisoft I would like to see again.

It's a very typical Ubisoft game, but the world is so immersive and beautifully crafted that I can't help but give it a positive review. It doesn't really feel like a traditional Assassin's Creed title, though the best entries in the series often break away from the formula. The gameplay can become repetitive, but exploring new regions of Egypt never loses its sense of wonder.

Jesus Christ this game is the definition of mid.

The story and characters are NOT interesting, despite what people say online. The open world is beautiful, but that's about it. 

Odyssey is just as bloated as this game but arguably executes on many of the same ideas better, though lots of fans will pretend to you that Origins somehow has better characters, less bloat or is the most pure of the AC RPG games. They are lying.

Assassin’s Creed Origins:

Is a strong entry in the series and a great starting point for the newer RPG-style Assassin’s Creed games.

The Ancient Egypt setting is easily one of the best parts of the game. Exploring deserts, pyramids, tombs, cities, rivers, and hidden locations feels amazing. The world looks great, the music fits the setting well, and Bayek is a strong main character. His story gives the game more emotion, and seeing the beginning of the Assassin Brotherhood makes it important for fans of the series.

The gameplay is fun, but it can be a bit of a grind. Origins changed the series a lot by adding more RPG elements, levels, gear, loot, abilities, and a bigger open world. I enjoyed the combat, exploration, story, and characters, but there are times where you need to do side content to keep up with the level requirements.

Going for 100% completion is fun and fairly easy, but it can still become a grind because there is a lot to clear on the map. If you enjoy open-world games and achievement hunting, it is not too bad, but it does take time.

I would recommend Assassin’s Creed Origins to casual players, achievement hunters, story players, and fans of Assassin’s Creed. It is not perfect, but it is a good game with a great world, strong main character, and fun gameplay.
Its a fun game but almost forces you do do external things in order to level up to make the next missions possible.

My favorite game forever and Bayek is the best protagonist ever with his voice actor being the best in the series. 
I dream about a sequel.

I wish Steam had a star or number rating system because I don't dislike or like this game - I find it...mid. Mid combat, mid story, mid movement, mid open world. All mid. It's not a terrible game but it's not an amazing game either. However, for this review, I'm going to lean on the side of not recommending it.

Gameplay is ok for what they are going for. despite all the weak game mechanics the Devs implemented, it is thanks to the amazing, this title made almost a perfect score in its final rating.

Rating:
9.5/10 & 4.8/5

Note:
The parkour and combat animation is severely lacking substantial finesse, with its driving force being only the story, making this title amazing. In the end, I can't score it a perfect 10 due to those issues.

The genre itself isn’t usually my cup of tea. I’ve never been a huge fan of stealth focused games, but Assassin’s Creed Origins won me over.
This is easily one of my favorite open worlds in gaming. Ancient Egypt is absolutely breathtaking, and exploring the map never got old. The world feels massive, diverse, and full of life, with every region having its own unique atmosphere.
One of the biggest selling points for me was the wildlife. Seeing different animals roaming made the world feel immersive and believable. It’s the kind of detail that makes exploration rewarding even when you’re not following a quest.
The environments are beautiful, the atmosphere is incredible, and simply traveling across the map can be an adventure on its own. Even as someone who doesn’t normally enjoy this genre, I found myself exploring and appreciating everything the world had to offer.
If you’re looking for a huge open world with a memorable setting, stunning landscapes, and plenty to discover, Assassin’s Creed Origins is definitely worth playing. It’s one of the few games that made me fall in love with a genre I usually don’t enjoy.

Assassin's Creed Origins will forever stay in my heart and be my favorite Assassin's Creed game ever. The world of Ancient Egypt is beautiful designed and felt real, the graphics is amazing. The story is good for the most part, but felt dull, same goes for many of the characters. I love the RPG games of Assassin's Creed, but that is also coming from someone who adore RPGs in general and I know many Assassin's Creed fans love the older ones that focuses on stealth. For the DLCs: The Hidden Ones is the best, beautiful story and locations. I also love the idea of The Curse of the Pharaohs, and that one is also made very well, but were the bosses in that DLC made by Hidetaka Miyazaki? Because ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

9/10!

Decided to pick it up on sale. Found the world beautiful, but the game play is dull. Figured I would be able to overlook the criticisms of AC games for the price. I was wrong.

This is probably a good game to pickup on a deep sale. Unfortunately, I'm not the target audience.

Do not recommend.
annoying combat but great game atmosphere and design

It's the first and the best of the "RPG-Creeds". The story is compelling, Egypt is gorgeous, the main character is likable.
I won't ask for more from post-Ezio Ubisoft lol.
very long aaa game won tlet me finish the story without doing a hundred same missions to levelup

One of the best games I ever played!

Most of the negative comments are from players who are scared to try the RPG AC games. I doubted at first because I had 100% completed all classic AC games. I could say that Origins still kept that OG AC vibe. The stealth is fun and challenging as the detection level is insane in this game. Egypt is also large and a beautiful map. I just loved everything about this game.

Now I'm playing Odyssey and I can say that Origins is still better xD

its not a bad game but its not the best game, without going in to deep into the lore its like this find this one guy to find another to find the main guy, along with the whole rpg setting to me it does not make sence how i cant silently kill someone bc im like 5 levels below its bs but oh well its ubisoft (PS get it when its on sale not full price)

mixed bag. the combat, sneaking, and very flexible climbing are all a great deal of fun, but spending this much time around primitive furry-worshipping doofuses sucks and it’s the brownest game i’ve played since the xbox 360 era

still more good than bad
Storytelling is fab, the game mechanics could be better but overall highly recommended.
A real fantastic immersion into history. Really enjoying thus far.

I have played almost every one of the games that came before this one (ezio trilogy, 3, 4, unity, syndicate) and this was the least fun i had. The game has a handfull of bug that took me out of the experience. I hated a lot of the mechanics that where created here, the fact that I need the grind in order to level up your hidden blade so you can kill people, or the fact that I had to do a handfull of sidemissions just so that I could do the main story. The combat mechanics are diffrent to what they were (wich is not a bad thing), but the tactic of parry then attack is still here (and in my opinion is the best in game). The game its self is not that bad, but as a assassin's creed game its disappointing.

Ubisoft launcher can't fix its mess.
Can't play this game anymore. 
Every company has to have their launcher to make them feel special. 
JUST LET ME PLAY THE GAME

10/10 game, one of my favorite of all time. I think this game is the perfect difficulty and length. It's got loads of my boxes ticked in a positive way and there is no way I can only play it once. The gear has more effects and variations than any Assassin's Creed game before it's time and the controls are a handful to learn on the first go, but well worth the learning curve for the options you get while battling. The only thing that was not my favorite was the grinding for materials to upgrade armor, hidden blade etc... If you put an hour or two into it, you should be fine in that regard.

Assassin’s Creed Odyssey has a great world and fun combat, but the level scaling completely ruins the pacing. If you don’t grind side missions constantly, enemies become damage sponges and your weapons feel useless. There were moments where my sword felt like it was doing zero damage, which just makes combat frustrating instead of rewarding. The game is enjoyable overall, but forcing players into side content just to stay viable is a terrible progression system.

This game is great!  Love the attention to detail, the historical accuracy (where possible) and the effort put into it.  Keep in mind it can get a little grindy at times, but they all relate either to the story or to history, so I didn't mind.
If you like any of the rpg AC games, this is more of the same... but in a semi-mythic Egypt this time.  If not, this won't do anything to change your mind.

Pretty good action adventure game during its time, graphics especially beautiful areas if your pc can take it, unlike the previous AC games, stealth wise not so much, we all gotta start somewhere I guess, community events must be adjusted to the dwindling players as well. also why have another launcher though, hopefully less developers force you into having to have another launcher in the future, still has some bugs years after launch, not game breaking but annoying. Overall worth a try, buy it during sale,
i love the world design, the story isn't that bad, but the jank, good lord the gameplay and combat is janky as hell.
If we talk about assassin's creed games, this is bad, but if we talk about a rpg game with great story set in ancient Egypt, this definitely wins.

It's Assassin's Creed, we all know what we're getting into with an entry into a series like this. Despite the Ubislop nature of it, I still enjoy it. The Egypt environments are freaking awesome and are the main reason I picked it up. Just getting to explore this massive open world, unlocking towers, fighting some dudes (with clunky combat mind you), seeing animals, idk it's just a nice time. The story I really don't care much about, the voice acting is quite bad and that just takes me out. Pick it up on sale and take a stroll through ancient Egypt, it's awesome for that.
Great game nice visuals and impressive storytelling

Really torn on this one. Dont like what it meant for the future of the franchise, but still appreciate the characters and the open world. Still a thumbs up for me if you ignore what damage it did in the long run.

Ancient Egypt is handled beautifully here: The deserts, the cities, the tombs, the pyramids, the Nile, all of it feels alive in a way that can easily pull you in for hours.
The story is actually pretty engaging too (not yet reaching the levels of the Ezio trilogy), and Bayek is a great protagonist, one of the few AC leads that feels genuinely memorable instead of just generic guy with a hidden blade.

The combat is also a big step up from the older games in a lot of ways. You have more weapon variety, more tools and more flexibility in how you approach fights, so even when things get messy it usually feels fun rather than stiff. Exploring tombs and ruins is probably the best part of the game for me, because it hits that perfect mix of mystery, atmosphere.
That said the game is not without its baggage and some of it is hard to ignore, for example the open world is huge but a lot of it is stuffed with filler that starts to feel like busywork after a while. Side quests can be interesting but far too many of them boil down to go here, kill this, fetch that, repeat until your XP bar stops being annoying. The level gating can also kill the pacing because sometimes the main story just pauses and tells you to go grind for a bit, which is never a great feeling in a game that already wants to be taken seriously.

Aaaand of course there is the usual Ubislop nonsense hanging around in the background: microtransactions, forced systems and the creeping feeling that some of the design is there to stretch playtime rather than improve the experience. The Assassin’s Creed identity is also a little watered down here so if you are attached to the old stealth and parkour formula this may feel more like a very good action RPG wearing AC branding than a true classic Assassin’s Creed game. Even the optimization can get a bit shaky, with occasional frame drops reminding you that the sand is not the only thing that is rough around the edges.

Still, despite all that, Origins succeeds because the good stuff is really good.
The setting is gorgeous, the atmosphere is strong, the combat is satisfying enough and the sense of adventure is hard to deny. It is messy, bloated and sometimes too eager to waste your time but it is also one of the most absorbing worlds Ubisoft ever made.

I would definitely recommend it when it gets on sale.
of all the new AC games I think this one has much better gameplay and graphics for different reasons it's tied with Odyssey for me. Great game. First of it's kind.
gameplay and combat is cool, but it parkour was too basic and it could have had more assassination animations, but overall its a pretty good game

This game is severely underrated. It is the best installment of the RPG era. Loved it a lot.

Generally a fun game. 
Would recommend to anyone who enjoys big open world side quest-like games.

9.5/10. I played this game a couple years ago when I went and played the whole AC Franchise from start to finish and this game blew my mind. I had played it before, but didn't finish it because I didn't like it enough, now this is my favorite AC game. 

My favorite part about it is definitely the beautifully detailed world, it is so insanely immersive, too, that for most of my playthrough I did not use fast travel and walked when going through cities/villages. I also definitely enjoyed the story of this game, even though I wouldn't say it's mind blowingly good. The gameplay aspect is simple and was satisfying for the whole game.

I also recommend the DLCs, the "The Hidden Ones" DLC is awesome when you're already into the story of AC and the "The curse of the pharaoh" DLC has beautiful environments!

Assassin's Creed Origins is a brilliant blend of the franchise's classic stealth roots and its modern RPG direction. The visuals are absolutely breathtaking, making you feel like you are on a virtual tour of ancient Egypt without ever leaving your house. With a compelling story, fantastic gameplay, and an incredible historical Discovery Tour, this game is a must-play.

Like all the AC games, this one is chock-full of little tid-bits and fantastic data and story and history! The views are beautifully scenic, the fighting is smooth, and there really isn't anything negative I have to say about his game. 10/10!
You' re telling me all this was  aprologue (4-5h in). love the game, insane graphics, Even better music.

The first time I played this game was on PlayStation. It was on sale so I figured I would add it to the collection. Of the RPG Assassins Creeds this one is the tightest with the best story. Smooth controls and excellent gameplay.
I cant create an account in obiseft but i think it would be really good if i can create account and actually play it

OML! Ima say that Assassin’s Creed Origins is a stunning reinvention of the series that absolutely nails its setting. Ancient Egypt feels alive in every detail & frm the sweeping deserts and bustling cities to the breathtaking pyramids on the horizon. Bayek's one of the most compelling protagonists in the franchise, and his journey adds real emotional weight to the exploration and combat. The RPG elements fit naturally, making progression feel meaningful without losing the classic Assassin’s Creed identity. Overall, it’s an immersive, beautifully crafted adventure that sets a new standard for historical open-world games!

Thanks for adding achievements.

The gameplay is different than other Assassin's Creed games which is good and bad. I liked the melee combat and dodging enemy attacks and archery was neat. The biggest issue is that enemies are now leveled (bullet sponge) at times. I should note they do now allow the hidden blade to be a one hit kill like previous Assassin's Creed games, which is nice. I truly do wish though they had not changed from previous games, the new engine isn't as fun as games like 3 or Brotherhood. You get to meet some historical people like Julius Caesar, Brutus, Cleopatra VII, Ptolemy XIII.

Pros:
- Cool new protagonist
- See how the Assassin Brotherhood was formed
- Beautiful environment
- You get to go inside of pyramids
- New present day protagonist, Layla Hassan
- Huge map
- Plenty of side quests
- Can now ride camels and chariots
- Discovery Tour to learn things

Cons:
- Your horse cannot swim
- Cannot switch playing between Bayek and Aya (like in Syndicate)
- More of an RPG experience with leveled enemies (bullet sponge)
- Not a lot of historical characters
- Game doesn't finish Cleopatra's story with Marc Antony (have to buy the comic series to know)

Well, here is the second game in my journey of completing all the Assassin's Creed games as close to 100% as possible in historical chronological order.

To start, I’m going to say that Assassin's Creed Origins seemed like a good game to me, but personally, I still prefer the charm of Assassin's Creed Odyssey. And I repeat, it’s a good game, and it has things that are done very, very well.

I think the best part of Origins is the first three hours and the last three hours. Why? Because the introduction seemed spectacular to me, absolute cinema. Very well done, very good pacing, everything was really well handled. It just flew by for me, unlike Odyssey, where even with side quests, world stuff and exploration, I spent 110 hours completing it, that’s including the DLCs and achievements tied to them, all that takes longer than the main game. But even so, it flew by. I completed the main story in only 30 hours.

Honestly, I had very high expectations regarding the story, since in the Assassin’s Creed community they label this game as the best of the mythology-era Assassin’s Creed games, so to speak. There are points that are good and others that are bad. I’ll explain them as this review goes on.

The world of Egypt seemed wonderful to me, really beautiful. It’s a much smaller map than Odyssey, but the recreation of ancient Egypt seemed 10 out of 10, especially because I love wandering through the desert. I wish they’d make a mod with one of the worms from Dune so I can feel like Lisan al-Gaib.

As for the story of Bayek of Siwa and Aya, I liked them. I liked their personalities and their motivations. They’re pretty clear characters, they’re well made.

Something I’d point out is that there’s tragedy after tragedy, after disappointment, after sadness, after mourning, after sorrow. I’m not saying that’s a bad thing, on the contrary, all the tragedy surrounding the entire game, all the misery surrounding Egypt, seemed very good to me, very well presented. What I’m saying is that there are scenes where it feels very exaggerated, and they could have changed the tone because it starts to feel parodic or satirical in certain missions, especially side quests.

But well, the story of the origin of Assassin's Creed, especially with its ending, even though it was very abrupt and short, seemed great to me. It's worth trying and worth playing to understand the origin story of the Brotherhood.

As for the gameplay and the items, it’s almost identical to Assassin's Creed Odyssey, with the difference in animations. The gameplay leans much more into violence and not so much into stealth, since Bayek of Siwa is a character who lets himself be carried away a lot by violence.

The gameplay, even though it’s comfortable, feels like the animations are less fluid than in the previous game, but even so, the choreography is pretty good. And also, when we face one of the bosses in the game and assassinate them, the assassination sequence, like in the other Assassin’s Creed games, where we’re transported inside the Animus, here it looks like an astral journey between a horror movie, mental paranoia, and the gods’ consolation, which seemed like a mix that I thought was wonderful.

And I like so much this effect they give to the assassinations, and it’s a shame that Odyssey didn’t have it. I would have liked being able to play more as Aya and customize her character more.

Speaking of customization, the cosmetics for each item also seem very well done and very stylish, although more limited than in the previous game.

Something I forgot to mention earlier in my review of Assassin's Creed Odyssey is the system Ubisoft implemented with Helix Credits. Now, to unlock outfits, to unlock cosmetics, to unlock weapons, to unlock upgrades, you have to pay with your wallet in dollars, like some kind of Fortnite battle pass.

And this, ladies and gentlemen, seems like complete fkin garbage to me, It’s horrible.

The soundtrack is pretty meh. There isn’t any memorable track beyond the main Assassin's Creed theme with Ezio Auditore da Firenze’s melody. But beyond that, it doesn’t have as good a soundtrack as Assassin's Creed Odyssey.

The present-day section, when we control Layla Hassan, which I understand also takes place before the events of Assassin's Creed Odyssey, but well, that’s a minor detail. We’ll piece the puzzle together later.

It didn’t seem as interesting or as good to me as the one in Odyssey, since in the previous game you could move around in several places, had more possibilities for parkour, could fight, and could do a lot more things. Even with the reports when you access the computer, the data, that part did seem interesting to me, and I think the reference to Aiden Pearce from Watch Dogs is excellent.

We can confirm that Watch Dogs and Assassin's Creed are part of the same universe. That seemed like an excellent nod to me.

Beyond the reports and the data related to Abstergo Industries, the character’s previous work, and the development of all the Assassin’s Creed lore, it seemed interesting to me. But beyond that, the present-day section didn’t seem as wow to me as the other one. But yeah, it flew by for me. Honestly. It has a pace that, to me, felt super accelerated, unlike Odyssey, which was much slower and took much more time developing everything.

Even so, I consider it a very good game. Unfortunately, I won’t be able to get the platinum because I don’t have the DLCs, and I’m not going to pay 20 dollars for DLC. So I’m going to settle for what I could do, as far as I could get, and say that I recommend it.

It’s a good game, but for now, the one I liked the most was Assassin's Creed Odyssey.
Great open world, although I don't really like how the hidden blade need to be scale up in order to assassinate enemies but overall good game

My first AC game, Origins is an action RPG and from my understanding was a reinvention from the traditional AC formula action/stealth. Generally heard good things about it and picked it up on deep discount to try out the franchise. Your character is a side quests (a kind of protector of the people) hunting the mysterious organization responsible for the death of his son.

The combat and stealth overall got it done but definitely felt a bit barebones at times and some elements felt clunky. I could never get the feel for the heavy attack and Chain Assassination skill felt very inconsistent (and assassinations could feel a tad slow I guess). Enemy variety largely boiled down to 'No Shield', 'Small Shield', 'Large Shield' and different weapon types.

Beyak is a good and interesting protaganist and some good chemistry and romance with his past wife Aya who has some moments where you play as. The story is overall pretty decent but has some rough spots. The side quests are a mixed bag while the main story started a bit choppy imo, got good in the middle then had bad pacing at the end (and a bland big bad + the final boss you don't even fight with your main character but with another one who has a fixed setup).

What the devs absolutely excelled and completely nailed was the setting. An absolutely incredible depiction of Ancient Egypt, the world feels immersive, dynamic and lived in, with NPCs reacting to you bumping into them. While many areas feel a bit copy/paste with similar objectives and enemies there's also interesting vistas, famous Egyptian sites to explore and a lust and beautiful crafted world. By far the biggest highlight for me.

8/10

If oddessy is an open world masterpiece with an unserious story, origins is the smaller, adult story version. far more dark, gritty, well written, and emotional. every mission feels important, and side quests are a joy too. not too long, not too complex. the map is still big, but not 500 hours big. combat is almost as good as oddessy, albeit less equipment based and simple. the visuals are almost as good as oddessy, and Egypt is an amazing setting [esp the pyramids]. bayik is a perfect main character with a vengeance plot. its really a flawless game for me.

Assassin's Creed Origins Review from someone who has 100%ed the main game and DLC's.

The game itself is beautiful. Being thrown into Egypt as a medjay is certainly a lot of fun. The story is amazing and you can't help but feel for Bayek and the supporting characters. If you're an old AC fan and wasn't sure due to the RPG elements, this would be your pick. It does delve into RPG elements, but mainly leveling and weapons. Dialogue isn't affected/outcome of story. Doing every icon on the map is super satisfying especially if you're a completionist. Bugs in this game, are little to minimal. Just stuff you'd expect from a Ubisoft game. But I will say this game is a lot more polished then some of the newer titles. [i] looking at you ac valhalla [/i] But I do heavily recommend this game especially if you're new to the AC franchise and curious to give it a try. It is a great starting game to begin your assassin journey. If you love Egypt, this game is for you as well. They have a discovery tour that takes you through the map and explains egyptian mythology and history. Very well done game. Now for the boring part:

Great Game Easily TOP 3 AC game of all time. And the best one out of the Sandbox Trilogy.

GOAT World Design , Great game, Great origin story, Great Pacing, Nice Combat and really fun side activities but the Main quest is short and there are a lot of side quest which really bored me out the side quests were good but really tiring. But collecting weapons sheilds and all were really fun.

Awesome controls really thought out as well.

I am in love with these games, these story and gameplay is amazing and not to mention the historical references! Absolute gem of a game 10/10 recommend playing. played it on PlayStation along with Odyssey. I recommend playing both of them. Those two are my favorite but You'll have your favorites!

Ok I have my own pros and cons for this game. firstly, it's a great game!! probably the best among the rpg trio, nice storyline, outstanding voice acting, balanced combat system and encouraging exploration through collectibles etc are some of the best part of a RPG game. However, the 100% completionists will suffer trying to get all from the game, since the map can be pretty tedious and the trials missions are a bit annoying with the matchup. Overall, it's a good game but it is not beating the allegation of being too grindy sometimes XD!!
The base gameplay is good, but the grind to all the collectables is a pain in the ass

Good world, fun stealth - one of the good assasins creed games.
Great visuals and exciting story! Really thrilling to fight so many different bosses.

Done everything from main q to both full dlcs main q, and i think made mistake bcs i started from Valhala to odessey to this, and mech of fighting fells ..... not finished , really unresponsive some times and really responsive other times that makes me really confused, and coming straight from odessey, overall i feel story is about worse as youre giong set path and you cant change it with youre own choses, BUT what kept me giong was world exploring as i really like doing it, and eve at 2026 this games grapfics are top notch
Playing it through for the THIRD time I'm still in love with it the way I was in 2017. Must play for an Ancient Egypt enthusiast like me

Feel like giving back to the community. If you’re thinking of getting Assassin's Creed Origins, I highly recommend it. I was in the same spot before, deciding between titles, but the detail in this game is really impressive. It rewards you a lot just for exploring the map, and there’s always something new to discover.
I am nervous as AC odessey is one of my favorites but so far this game is stunning and the leveling system is very balanced

1. Bayek of Siwa is one of the most deep and badass characters I have ever played. His actor did a phenomenal job. He can be joyful and happy in one cutscene, sad and cry in the other, and rage and kill like a god. The range of emotions are insane.
2.  Egypt is gorgeous in this game. Ubisoft really knows how to make the most beautiful worlds in video games. Every place has its own story and feeling to it. Every NPC has its own daily routine and you can use it to your own advantage, The game world has cool depth to it.

One of the best games I have ever played. I wish the other RPG AC games had followed steps of this game.

Many boring missions, and the worst thing so far is that, I get into to this horrible scene, red screen, two rows of fire, a road downward, a tomb, a big snake, and no hud, can't go back or quit, WTF. you sick morons. not funny at all. I LOVE Egypt, so I was really looking forward to playing this game. And what a disappointment it was...
The game is BORING. I still have not finished the game, even after getting back to is like 3 times already...
The story sucks, some parts of the map are literally empty, basically, 1 hour in, you have seen every activity type in the game. And after that, it is just grinding the "?" marks.
